chapter.title: Deploying selfinstall images

info.meta: Learn how to deploy SUSE Linux Micro using self-install images and configure the system on first boot

abstract.para: The chapter describes deployment of &slema; from selfinstall pre-built ISO images.

note.title: Installation using the selfinstall ISO image does not create a boot EFI entry
note.para: During the deployment of the selfinstall ISO, the image of the system is just copied to the selected disk, therefore, an EFI boot entry is not created (like it normally would if the system is deployed using an installer). You might need to manually boot your system using the EFI shell by selecting the &slema; boot loader. After the first boot, you can use efibootmgr to create the boot entry. efibootmgr is available by default in the deployed image.

chapter.para: When deploying &productnameshort; using the pre-built selfinstall ISO, proceed as follows:

step.para: If needed, plug in the configuration device.
Boot your machine with the selfinstall ISO attached.
Select Install SLE Micro to start the installation process.
Select the disk where &slema; will be installed and confirm that you want to delete data on the disk. A &slema; image is then copied to the disk.
Using kexec, your system reboots and is then prepared for the configuration process.
Start the configuration process by selecting SLE Micro. &slema; is either configured according to the instructions provided on the configuration device, or jeos-firstboot is started.
(Optional) Configure your system with jeos-firstboot:
Confirm the configuration.
Select your keyboard layout and confirm your selection.
Read the license agreement and accept it.
Select your time zone.
Enter a password for root.
Confirm the root password.
Accept the statement about SLE Micro registration.
After the first boot, you can register your &slema; instance by using the transactional-update command. For details, refer to .
&slema; has extensions available. To use some of these extensions, you need to add the extension to your subscription from the installed system. For details, refer to .

section.title: Preparing the configuration device
section.para: The following procedure describes how to prepare the configuration device:

step.para: Format the disk to any file system supported by SLE Micro: Ext3, Ext4, etc.:

Set the device label to either ignition (when either Ignition or Combustion is used) or combustion (when only Combustion is used). If needed, for example on Windows host system, you can use uppercase letters for the labels, too. To label the device, run:
You can use any type of configuration storage media that your virtualization system or your hardware supports: ISO image, a USB flash disk, etc.
Mount the device:
Create the directory structure as mentioned in or , depending on the configuration tool used:
or:

Prior to booting for the first time, prepare all elements of the configuration that will be used by or . To log in to your system, you need to provide a password for root or set up passwordless authentication, otherwise the system will not be accessible after the first boot.

section.title: Minimal initial configuration
section.para: If you do not attach any configuration medium, jeos-firstboot is started, and you can perform a minimal configuration of your system as follows:

step.para: Confirm the configuration.
Select your keyboard layout and confirm your selection.
Read the license agreement and accept it.
Select your time zone.
Enter a password for &rootuser;.
Confirm the &rootuser; password.
Accept the statement about &slema; registration.
